title: hieracium phlomoides subsp. phlomoides
intro: Hieracium phlomoides subsp. phlomoides, also known as Hawkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family, characterized by its yellow flowers and bitter milky sap. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Hawkweed include reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and promoting wound healing, due to its antimicrobial and antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, Hawkweed is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although its use should be supervised by a healthcare professional due to potential toxicity.
